title: No legal action if infiltrators go back on their own: Amit Shah
content: Union government committed to identifying and deporting every single infiltrator from the country, says Home Minister

title: Govt. plans security protocol for fishing harbours under CISF oversight
content: A senior government official says the CISF will design a security template and guide the local administration in managing the security protocol. There are 1,547 notified fish landing centres and fishing harbours in 13 coastal States and UTs

title: Preparation, not fear will combat El Nino: Agriculture Minister Chouhan
content: El Nino is not something to be feared but something that we must prepare for, says Minister at inauguration of the Kharif Conference in Delhi

title: Twisha death case: Retired judge Giribala Singh arrested by CBI
content: CBI officers arrived at Giribala Singh’s residence and carried out an investigation; the agency plans to confront Ms. Singh and her son Samarth Singh, Twisha’s husband, with each other’s statements

title: Go by value proposition TG offers, refrain from incentive shopping: Industries official
content: Commissioner/Director of Industries Nikhil Chakravarthi who was speaking at a conference on data centres also urged firms to look beyond Hyderabad, wanted them to help promote backward linkages to spur manufacturing

title: NHAI identifies 1600 kms for monetisation for FY 27
content: The identified projects are across Haryana, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Maharashtra.

title: PM should have sacked Pradhan for ‘ruining the future of lakhs of students’: Rahul Gandhi
content: Mr. Gandhi asks whether any background check was conducted into the controversial company that won the contract for the CBSE OSM process, accuses Mr. Pradhan of being “complicit”

title: The Hindu, Galgotias University establish Centre for Excellence in Journalism
content: Select students of Galgotias University will gain access to internship opportunities with The Hindu’s editorial teams, collaborative publishing opportunities for visual communication projects, curated workshops, field visits, and structured interventions by media professionals.

title: Watch: Karnataka CM change: Siddaramaiah resigns, what’s next? | Above the Fold | 28.05.2026
content: From Siddaramaiah’s resignation and the Congress leadership transition in Karnataka to India-China talks over the LAC and the Madras High Court’s cow slaughter ban ahead of Bakrid, and more.

title: Panel on ‘artificial’ demographic changes may consider need for law to tackle issue: Amit Shah
content: If illegal migrants leave on their own, BJP government in West Bengal will refrain from filing legal cases against them and facilitate their departure, says Home Minister

title: Indian Air Force likely to oversee transport of NEET-UG re-test papers
content: This measure follows the cancellation of the original examination after an alleged question paper leak

title: Id prayers shifted to Brigade Ground in Kolkata as Bengal govt. imposes fresh curbs on Bakrid celebrations
content: Morning namaz moved from Red Road to Brigade Parade Ground this year, while public animal slaughter and roadside festivities remain prohibited under the new rules of the BJP government in the State; Hindu cow owners complain of economic distress due to sluggish cattle sale.

title: Left parties plan ‘rasta roko’ on June 9 against hike in fuel prices
content: They accuse the Central and State governments of failing to reduce taxes and pursuing anti-people policies

title: Lord Govindaraja rides Hanumantha and Gaja vahanams on sixth day
content: Artistes performing Chekka Bhajana and Kolatam, and ‘Prabandha Goshti’ form a part of the grand processions

title: Centre extended valuable support for Amaravati and Polavaram projects, says A.P. CM
content: Mahanadu is a grand success as 16.78 lakh people have participated in the programme held in hybrid mode, says Naidu and assures that worthy cadres will be duly elevated

title: TDP rooted in principles laid down by NTR, says Rammohan Naidu
content: The party is committed to the welfare of the Backward Classes and weaker sections, and will ensure the Central programmes and funds reach them, says the Civil Aviation Minister; several projects taking tangible shape due to the cooperation of the Centre, says Pemmasani Chandra Sekhar

title: Fuel price surge, West Asia crisis: Karnataka Travels owners’ association seeks urgent economic relief measures
content: Karnataka State Travels Owners’ Association says that the State government earns approximately ₹23,000 crore to ₹24,000 crore annually through VAT and sales tax on petrol and diesel, with the current petrol VAT rate standing at nearly 29.84%

title: No ‘double standards’ in fight against terrorism, says NSA Ajit Doval in Russia
content: The National Security Adviser participated in the first International Security Forum and the 14th Meeting of High Representatives for Security Matters in Moscow

title: After smooth transition, new Karnataka CM has a delicate task ahead
content: One of the biggest challenges before the new Chief Minister would be balancing his relationship with Siddaramaiah’s loyalists and support base within the Congress

title: CPI(M) urges Tamil Nadu govt to appeal against HC order banning cow slaughter
content: A summer vacation Bench comprising of Justices G. R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan had directed the Chief Secretary and the Director General of Police/Head of Police Force (DGP/HoPF) to ensure no cow or calf is slaughtered anywhere in Tamil Nadu either on the eve of Bakrid or on any other day.

title: Veerendra Kumar was among the first in Kerala to talk about environmental conservation: CM Satheesan
content: Were inspired by the late socialist leader’s activities, including the agitation that eventually forced Coca Cola to close down its Plachimada plant in Palakkad, the CM said

title: Velankanni-bound tourist bus overturns near Nellore, 16 injured
content: A private bus carrying 43 pilgrims to Velankanni hit a highway railing and overturns near Chintareddypalem; three suffer serious injuries

title: Shutdown hits Id in Arunachal capital region
content: An indigenous youth organisation called the bandh demanding action against illegal structures, including mosques, and ejection of undocumented migrants

title: Regional parties' income falls 52% in 2024-25; 21 spent more than they earned: ADR report
content: Among regional parties, the highest income was declared by the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) at ₹228.31 crore, constituting 19.14% of the total income, followed closely by the All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) and the YSR Congress

title: Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan takes responsibility for CBSE answer sheet discrepancies
content: CBSE working on a solution to OSM concerns, says Minister, noting that almost 40 crore answer sheet pages were scanned under the new system; chairs review of evaluation and post-exam process

title: Protest planned against SIR process in Bidar tomorrow
content: As many as 39 organisations, including minority, Dalit and backward class groups, will participate in the Karnataka Pratirodha protest

title: Chinna Bompalle tank receives water after 20 years
content: District Collector attributes it to the feeder channel works taken up under the ‘Jaladhara Jalaharathi’ programme

title: Expert team continues inspection of Polavaram project for second day
content: It focuses on the borrow earth being used for the Earth-cum-Rock Fill dam works, examines soil storage areas and quality tests being conducted before its usage

title: State witnessing unprecedented development, says Bharath
content: Participating in the second day of ‘Mahanadu’ programme, the Industries Minister assures that he is working tirelessly for the development of Kurnool

title: Shutters down on 458 UG programmes in government first-grade colleges
content: There are a total of 440 government first-grade colleges under Collegiate Education Department in the State, and there is zero enrollment for a total of 1,091 undergraduate combinations in these colleges for the year 2025-26

title: Caste survey: Siddaramaiah's parting shot for DKS, Rahul
content: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah's last-minute acceptance of the caste census report presents a political dilemma for successor DK Shivakumar. The report, potentially validating the strength of backward classes, could alienate dominant communities. This also challenges Rahul Gandhi's national push for caste data, risking accusations of hypocrisy if not acted upon.

title: Super Sukhoi programme: Modernising IAF's work horse fighter fleet
content: The Indian Air Force is significantly upgrading its Sukhoi-30MKI fleet, a massive Rs 63,000 crore project. This 'Super Sukhoi' initiative will enhance avionics, sensors, and mission systems, including a new indigenous AESA radar and AI-powered computers.

title: 'We don’t want what happened to PBKS': Jofra Archer keeps RR grounded
content: Jofra Archer's fiery spell dismantled Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL 2026 Eliminator, propelling Rajasthan Royals towards the final. Archer, pleased with the team's bowling effort, highlighted the importance of powerplay wickets.  A remarkable 97 off 29 balls by 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi set a formidable target, showcasing RR's strong form as they prepare to face Gujarat Titans.

title: Gulf of suspicion: US and Iran said to be nearing deal even as missiles fly
content: Amidst escalating tensions and attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, the US and Iran are reportedly nearing a diplomatic breakthrough. A draft Memorandum of Understanding could extend a fragile ceasefire, opening a 60-day window for talks on Iran's nuclear program in exchange for economic relief.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: IPL autopsy: How the Sooryavanshi storm ended SRH’s title dream after dramatic revival
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad staged a remarkable IPL 2026 turnaround, becoming a feared batting unit. Despite a strong league run, their title hopes were dashed by RR opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sensational knock.  The team's reliance on an explosive top order and lack of Powerplay wickets ultimately proved their undoing in the playoffs.

title: JP Morgan responds to order to pay $4M to ex-employee fired over home business meeting
content: A Wall Street arbitration panel has ordered JP Morgan to pay former employee Ryan Bodner $4 million in damages, plus interest, for wrongful termination. Bodner was fired over an expense submission for a business meeting at his home, which the bank allegedly mischaracterized as a party. The panel also recommended expunging the reason for his termination from his record.

title: What happens when you stop paying your home loan EMIs? Here's what the bank can do
content: Missing home loan EMIs triggers a legal process, potentially leading to property auction. After 90 days of non-payment, your loan becomes an NPA. Lenders then issue a 60-day demand notice under SARFAESI Act. Failure to pay allows banks to take possession and auction the property, but borrowers can settle dues anytime before sale completion.

title: Jannik Sinner collapses in Paris heat, crashes out of French Open in second-round shock
content: World number one Jannik Sinner dramatically crashed out of the French Open after a stunning physical collapse in the Paris heat.  Leading by two sets, Sinner faltered under 32-degree temperatures, losing 18 consecutive points and ultimately the match to Juan Manuel Cerundolo. This shocking exit ends his 30-match winning streak and opens up the men's draw.

title: As EU fines China's Temu $200M , European regulator 'warns' its 130M users
content: European Union has slapped a €200 million fine on Chinese online retailer Temu for failing to prevent the sale of illegal products. Regulators found Temu's risk assessment systems inadequate, exposing millions of European users to dangerous goods. Temu disputes the ruling, calling the penalty excessive and outdated, while the EU emphasizes this is a strong message on risk management.

title: Labour codes and nomination processes: What employees need to know about securing their benefits
content: Nomination assumes greater importance in the context of gratuity, given that it is a long-term benefit and often represents a significant financial entitlement.

title: 'We were gagged first, now there is pressure to defend it': Inside CBSE’s OSM crisis
content: CBSE’s On-Screen Marking (OSM) controversy has exposed growing distrust within India’s examination system, with teachers alleging they were effectively “gagged” through a March 16 advisory warning against sharing evaluation-related experiences online. Evaluators claim they faced blurry scans, missing supplementary pages, server failures and marking difficulties during Class 12 assessments but feared disciplinary action if they raised concerns publicly. The controversy has now deepened further amid allegations that schools and educators were later informally encouraged to publicly defend the OSM system as criticism intensified nationwide.

title: 20 years in jail, Rs 34cr blood money: Kerala man on Saudi death row returns home on Bakrid
content: After two decades in a Saudi prison, Feroke native Abdul Rahim returned home on Eid Al Adha, a moment of immense joy for his family and the Malayali community. A massive crowdfunding effort raised Rs 47 crore, including Rs 34 crore in blood money, to secure his release from a death sentence and subsequent 20-year imprisonment.

title: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah’s Delhi flight diverted to Jaipur amid heavy rain
content: A special flight carrying Karnataka leader Siddaramaiah and several other Congress dignitaries was diverted to Jaipur due to adverse weather in Delhi. The group was en route to the capital for a meeting with the party high command following Siddaramaiah's resignation.

title: Chandrayaan-2 finds strong evidence for ice in 'doubly-shadowed' lunar craters
content: India's Chandrayaan-2 has detected strong evidence of ice buried deep within permanently shadowed craters at the Moon's south pole. This groundbreaking discovery, made using radar data, could be crucial for future human missions, offering a vital local resource for drinking water and rocket fuel. The findings highlight the south pole's significance in the ongoing space race.

title: Amit Shah says panel on ‘artificial’ demographic change will examine need for new law
content: A high-level committee, tasked with examining "artificial" demographic shifts due to illegal immigration, will also assess the need for a new law. Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated the panel, headed by Justice Prakash Prabhakar Naoleker, will report within a year.

title: ‘Best buy in IPL history’: Lalit Modi’s massive verdict on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, at just 15, is being hailed as IPL's greatest signing. He smashed a 97 off 29 balls in an Eliminator, pushing RR to Qualifier 2. The teenager now holds the record for most sixes in an IPL season with 65 and is the first to score 600 runs before turning 16.

title: ‘Ranji performances ignored by selectors’: Lad calls IPL ‘shortcut’ to Indian team
content: Veteran cricketer Siddhesh Lad expresses concern over the current state of Indian cricket selection. He states that consistent performance in the Indian Premier League, not the Ranji Trophy, is now the benchmark for national team selection. Lad feels this devalues domestic cricket and discourages promising young players from focusing on longer formats.

title: India, Australia forge defence ties to check China’s Indo‑Pacific ambitions
content: India and Australia are forging a robust defence partnership to counter China's Indo-Pacific assertiveness.  Focusing on practical cooperation, they've inked a logistics agreement for reciprocal base access and expanded joint military exercises.  Enhanced maritime domain awareness and defence technology collaboration are key, alongside army-to-army engagements. This deepening alliance aims to ensure a multipolar and stable region.

title: ‘Matter of pride’: Sooryavanshi’s priceless reaction to Tendulkar’s praise
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi received high praise from Sachin Tendulkar for his IPL 2026 performance. The young cricketer scored 97 runs off 29 balls, helping Rajasthan Royals win. Tendulkar lauded Sooryavanshi's batting technique. This recognition is a significant motivator for the 15-year-old. Rajasthan Royals will now play Gujarat Titans for a spot in the final.

title: 4 of family, including former sarpanch, burnt alive inside vehicle in Ajmer
content: Tragedy struck Ajmer's Shrirampura village as four family members, including a former sarpanch and his wife, perished in a vehicle fire. While initially appearing accidental, police now suspect foul play, citing injury marks and bloodstains at the residence. The investigation is focusing on the former sarpanch's family dynamics.

title: Rain lashes parts of Delhi-NCR; IGI airport issues travel advisory
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a sudden weather shift with rain and severe thunderstorms, prompting a red alert from the IMD. Wind speeds are expected to reach 80 km/h, with the activity continuing until May 31. This follows days of extreme heat, with temperatures dropping significantly. Experts attribute the change to a western disturbance and moisture from the Bay of Bengal.

title: Twisha death probe: CBI arrests mother-in-law Giribala Singh after HC scraps anticipatory bail
content: The CBI has taken Giribala Singh, mother-in-law of deceased Twisha Sharma, into custody after the Madhya Pradesh High Court quashed her anticipatory bail. The court cited insufficient appreciation of evidence, including witness statements and WhatsApp chats, and noted her alleged lack of cooperation with the investigation into dowry harassment and cruelty allegations.

title: Woman who sued Trump over sexual assault allegation faces DOJ investigation: Report
content: Federal prosecutors in Chicago are investigating E. Jean Carroll for potential perjury during her lawsuits against Donald Trump. The probe centers on whether she falsely stated she received no outside financial support for her legal battles. This emerged after it was revealed a billionaire entrepreneur's nonprofit helped cover her expenses, sparking a dispute over disclosure.

title: Khamenei sidelined? Iran's Supreme leader not involved in US peace talks: Report
content: Questions are mounting over Supreme Leader Khamenei's direct involvement in Iran's ongoing US negotiations, with reports citing "serious ambiguities" about coordination. This uncertainty follows trips by Iranian officials and a reluctance to hold talks in Tehran or Pakistan, fueling speculation about who is directing the diplomatic process amid heightened Gulf tensions and ongoing clashes.

title: ‘Change team’: Ex-India cricketer asks Yashasvi Jaiswal to leave Rajasthan Royals
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal, once seen as India's next batting star, faces inconsistent white-ball opportunities and is overshadowed by his IPL partner Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. Former cricketer Ambati Rayudu suggests Jaiswal needs a team change, proposing Mumbai Indians as a logical destination for his development.

title: Gujarat student stabbed to death in Canada; family informed after 12 days
content: A young Indian student, Vidhi, living in Canada for higher studies and aiming for permanent residency, was found murdered in the Niagara region. She was reportedly killed in a knife attack, with her family in Gujarat informed nearly 12 days later. Canadian authorities have launched a murder investigation.

title: Vinesh’s comeback hits fresh roadblock as WFI moves SC against Delhi HC order
content: Olympian Vinesh Phogat faces a new challenge as the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) appeals to the Supreme Court, questioning her eligibility for the 2026 Asian Games trials. The WFI argues the Delhi High Court order allowing her participation was premature, citing retirement and missed test allegations.  The federation insists selection is merit-based, not on special exemptions.

title: 'High command asked me': Karnataka's longest-serving CM Sidda resigns
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has resigned, paving the way for Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar to take the top post. This transition follows months of speculation and high-level meetings in Delhi.  Photos showcasing unity between the leaders were shared, signaling an amicable power handover after the Congress high command's directive for a leadership change.

title: 'My biggest discovery was that ...': Quote of the day by Viola Davis
content: Viola Davis, the illustrious Oscar-winning actress, shares her transformative journey in the memoir 'Finding Me.' She candidly discusses how life's obstacles can serve as stepping stones to redefine who we are. By reflecting on her past struggles, she illustrates that strength emerges from adversity, fostering resilience and a profound capacity for love.

title: US arsenal depleted? Restocking THAAD, Tomahawk & Patriot missiles could take years
content: A recent Iran conflict has significantly depleted US high-end missile stockpiles, raising alarms in Washington. Critical systems like Tomahawks and Patriots may take years to replenish, creating a 'window of vulnerability' amid rising tensions with China. Production bottlenecks and allied demands complicate rebuilding efforts, impacting future defense readiness.

title: Why Kolkata continues to rank as India’s safest city
content: Kolkata has once again been named India's safest metro city, maintaining its top ranking for the fifth consecutive year according to the NCRB 2024 report. Despite a recent disturbing incident, the city recorded the lowest rate of cognisable offences per lakh population, showcasing a long-term decline in crime.

title: Sooryavanshi's three-word post goes viral after his brutal knock against SRH
content: In a stunning display of talent, the young prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi showcased his prowess by scoring an electrifying 97 runs off just 29 balls. This performance almost set a new benchmark, falling just short of Chris Gayle's record for the fastest century in IPL history. With an astonishing 65 sixes this season, he has also eclipsed Gayle’s previous record.

title: India, Singapore discuss deeper strategic cooperation at 16th Defence Policy Dialogue
content: India and Singapore solidified their robust defence partnership during the 16th Defence Policy Dialogue in Singapore. Discussions focused on deepening cooperation in strategic areas, including emerging and digital warfare domains. The visit also explored collaboration opportunities in advanced digital and technical capabilities, reinforcing the strong bilateral ties guided by their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.

title: AAP leader Jai Pal Singh Bau shot after parking dispute in Amritsar
content: Aam Aadmi Party's Ward No. 42 in-charge, Jai Pal Singh Bau, was shot in the leg following a heated argument with his neighbour over vehicle parking outside his Amritsar residence. He is stable and undergoing treatment at a private hospital. CCTV footage captured the escalating verbal dispute. Police are investigating the incident.

title: Rs 1L awarded to patient who underwent two kidney stone surgeries but got no relief
content: A Delhi surgeon and hospital have been held liable for medical negligence after a patient endured persistent kidney stone pain despite two surgeries. The consumer commission upheld the earlier order, directing them to pay compensation.  An expert opinion found it unlikely for such a large stone to reform so quickly, rejecting the hospital's defense.

title: Sooryavanshi tipped to hit '100 sixes' in an IPL season after shattering Gayle's record
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has rewritten IPL history, smashing Chris Gayle's record for most sixes in a season with 65. His explosive 97 off 29 balls propelled Rajasthan Royals to Qualifier 2. Commentator Aakash Chopra lauded his power and maturity, predicting he could one day reach an astonishing 100 sixes in a single IPL season.

title: The Exact Method Illegal Online Betting Networks Use To Cash In On IPL
content: From Telegram links and WhatsApp forwards to direct promotional calls, betting platforms are aggressively targeting users during the IPL season, luring them in with bonus offers, referral schemes and easy sign-ups

title: TVK Laps Up More AIADMK Dissidents, Minister's '90% Cadre In A Month' Warning
content: TVK hadn't made any overtures to the turncoats, and they joined without being prompted, said a senior party leader.

title: Retired IAS Officer Dies In Delhi House Fire, AC Blast Suspected: Cops
content: An 80-year-old retired IAS officer died while his son sustained injuries after a fire broke out at a house in south Delhi's Hauz Khas area, police said on Thursday.

title: Inside CBI's "Tunnel View" Mapping Of Twisha Sharma's Final Hours
content: Through this digital reconstruction, the CBI will try to trace Twisha Sharma's last movements, identify who entered or exited different areas and establish the exact sequence of events before the incident.

title: Trinamool MP Accuses Colleague Of Misogyny, He Hits Back With Probe Threat
content: Trinamool MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar's hints about misbehaviour from a party colleague, mentioned in her resignation letter, became public today with her letter to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, seeking permission to file a complaint against MP Kalyan Banerjee.

title: "Trinamool Will Be Finished In A Few Days": Party MP's Explosive Claim
content: The latest leader to speak up is Trinamool Congress's Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Shekhar Roy, who, among other things, had predicted a disintegration of the party.

title: Netanyahu Orders Israel Army To Take Control Of 70% Of Gaza Strip
content: Gaza remains gripped by daily violence, with both the Israeli military and Hamas accusing one another of violating the truce in effect since October 10.

title: US Forces In War Zones Being Targeted Using Location Data: Report
content: The disclosure was the first official confirmation that US forces had been targeted in an active war zone, a letter sent on Thursday to the Pentagon said.

title: Meghalaya Reviewing Civil Code Impact After Assam Passes Bill
content: The political discourse surrounding the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) has gained momentum in Meghalaya after the Assam Assembly cleared the UCC Bill, prompting renewed discussions over its possible implications for tribal states in the Northeast.

title: Opinion: DK Shivakumar Comes With Mastery Of Delhi Networks And Karnataka Grassroots
content: It will be a true test for DK Shivakumar, the eight-time MLA and strongman from Kanakapura, as he navigates an out-of-power Siddaramaiah and manages Karnataka's delicate socio-political equations

title: US, Israel Want To Bring Iran To Knees To Compensate For Defeat: Mojtaba
content: Khamenei's address marks the anniversary of the founding of Iran's national legislature. The Supreme Leader has not appeared in public since before he took office in March.

title: Instagram Influencer Attempts Suicide, Mother Claims Fiance Created Fake Profile
content: Ronisha Satti's mother said Prashant had taken over Rs 10 lakh from the influencer and reneged on his promise to marry her.

title: Woman Declared Dead, Father, Brother Arrested For Murder. She Turns Up Alive
content: Shivani Kalmekar went missing in April from Burhanpur in Madhya Pradesh. She was declared dead by Maharashtra police, who arrested her father and brother for allegedly murdering her.

title: Opinion: Blog | A Star Is Born: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's Unreal Rise Amid India's Search For Continuity
content: Indian cricket badly needs a new star. Mahendra Singh Dhoni is now done, while Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma are nearing the end. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi could well be on his way to becoming India's next big brand.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Strong Dust Storm, Rains Likely In Delhi Shortly, Says Weatherman
content: Weather Updates Today, Heatwave, IMD Alert LIVE Updates: Light to very light rain is expected during the afternoon and evening, accompanied by strong winds and squalls reaching speeds of 50-60 kmph.

title: After Over 4 Decades, Haryana BJP Gets Its Second Woman Chief
content: The Haryana unit of the BJP on Thursday got its second woman president after more than four decades when the party appointed Archana Gupta as its new chief.

title: Japan Bans Indian Mangoes After 20 Years. Food Officials Found This
content: Japan follows a zero-tolerance policy for invasive pests like fruit flies, which are considered a major threat to domestic agriculture.

She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More

title: LeT turns on its Pakistan masters over recognising Israel
content: Raj Shekhar Jha is a journalist for the Times of India with over a decade of experience in reporting on national security, terrorism, crime and prisons.

content: Aashish Saxena is an Assistant News Editor at the Times of India. He has 14 years of editing and reporting experience in both print and digital media. He writes extensively on geopolitics, Indian politics, defence tech, and current affairs in South Asia and Middle East. Read More

title: Govt may use IAF aircraft to transport question papers for NEET retest: Report
